name: Shad Collins
abstract: Lester Rallingston "Shad" Collins (June 27, 1910 – June 6, 1978) was an American jazz trumpet player, composer and arranger, who played in several leading bands between the 1930s and 1950s, including those led by Chick Webb, Benny Carter, Count Basie, Lester Young, Cab Calloway and Sam "The Man" Taylor.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: My recording activities started the 7th of July when I recorded with Dicky Wells' Orchestra which included Bill Dillard, Shad Collins...

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: And I went with Cab. I played baritone in this band... Jonah Jones and Doc Cheatham and Shad Collins were the trumpets.

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Shad joined us in 1938... We had Buck Clayton, Ed Lewis, Shad Collins, and Harry Edison.

about_quotes.speaker: Russell Procope
about_quotes.evidence: we had Shad Collins, (Richard) a trumpet player. Bill Dillard and Dizzy.

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Cozy Cole) does not speak in the provided context. The interviewer mentions Shad Collins as a trumpet player in Cab Calloway's band who played solos, but there is no direct interaction or personal relationship described between Cozy Cole and Shad Collins.

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: In the band is about . . . what? There’s ten men on the stage. No, eight men on the stage, and the rest of them is all late. ... When I look around, Shad Collins, here he come. He sneak on.
about_quotes.doc_title: Danny Barker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
